raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.0,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":0.3,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.0,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The delegation is an exclusive logical-consistency audit; load-bearing because failure to flag invalid inferences (e.g., AUC 0.89 stated as sufficient to conclude ±0.5-point accuracy preservation — a ranking metric does not directly bound downstream accuracy; and the portability claim that 'no labeled difficulty information' is required contradicts the training section which constructs a 480k supervised binary-label corpus) directly invalidates the delegation. Cross-section coherence must also be checked: the 71% S-acceptable rate, class balancing, threshold tau, and the 64%/36% dispatch split must be jointly consistent, as must the arithmetic in the cost analysis (0.64×(1/20)+0.36×1 ≈ 0.392, consistent with the stated 60% reduction)."},"clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":""}

rationale.R1.2: The delegation is an exclusive logical-consistency audit; load-bearing because failure to flag invalid inferences (e.g., AUC 0.89 stated as sufficient to conclude ±0.5-point accuracy preservation — a ranking metric does not directly bound downstream accuracy; and the portability claim that 'no labeled difficulty information' is required contradicts the training section which constructs a 480k supervised binary-label corpus) directly invalidates the delegation. Cross-section coherence must also be checked: the 71% S-acceptable rate, class balancing, threshold tau, and the 64%/36% dispatch split must be jointly consistent, as must the arithmetic in the cost analysis (0.64×(1/20)+0.36×1 ≈ 0.392, consistent with the stated 60% reduction).
